mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in real retail sales reflects persistent consumer demand that translates directly into higher corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns continuously without requiring a threshold breach.
failure_condition: 20-day forward return following month-over-month RSAFS increases is not significantly greater than zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0% imposes restrictive financial conditions by sharply raising discount rates and corporate debt-service burdens, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0% during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rising unemployment and falling industrial production identify a synchronized contraction in labor and supply conditions that amplifies recession risk beyond either signal alone, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between UNRATE changes and INDPRO changes fails to produce a statistically significant negative coefficient (p > 0.05) in a 20-day forward return regression over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions characterized by elevated interest rates, sustained weakness in housing starts signals that monetary restraint has constrained the interest-rate-sensitive construction sector, foreshadowing a broader demand pullback and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with elevated interest rates
failure_condition: HOUST declines are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of late-cycle months identified by concurrent FEDFUNDS > 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month drops in real retail sales immediately foreshadow near-term corporate earnings misses, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week as markets swiftly reprice growth expectations.
failure_condition: RSAFS monthly drops > 1.5% are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unexpected accelerations in payroll employment growth above 400 thousand over three months signal strengthening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 returns as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: PAYEMS accelerations above 400k over 3 months are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When restrictive monetary policy coincides with rising unemployment, the dual pressure from higher discount rates and weakening labor income creates a non-linear negative wealth effect that drives significant negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and UNRATE increases > 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Joint occurrence of FEDFUNDS > 4% and rising UNRATE fails to produce negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns (t-test p > 0.05) over any 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth above 2.5% annualized signals robust aggregate demand that supports corporate top-line expansion, predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as macro momentum persists.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth > 2.5% q/q annualized is followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 40% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.
